latest.title: Conversion of B Shares
latest.content:
12 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 3034W
Tungsten West PLC
12 March 2026
12 March 2026
Tungsten West Plc
("Tungsten West" or the "Company")
Conversion of B Shares
Tungsten West (AIM: TUN), the mining company focused on restarting production at the Hemerdon tungsten and tin mine in Devon in the UK, announces that on 6 and 11 March 2026, the Company received notices for the partial conversion of B Shares into new Ordinary Shares. The Company has received the following conversion notices from:
·
Drakewood Capital Management Limited to convert 10,339,891 B Shares into new Ordinary Shares on a one-to-one basis; and
·
Henry Maxey to convert 23,466,395 B Shares into new Ordinary Shares on a one-to-one basis.
Accordingly, the Company will issue an aggregate of 33,806,286 new Ordinary Shares on a one-to-one basis. Following the conversion, the number of outstanding B Shares not yet converted into Ordinary Shares will be 257,075,833.
Admission and Total Voting Rights
Applications have been made for the new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission"). Admission is expected to become effective on 17 March 2026.
Immediately following Admission, the issued ordinary share capital of the Company is expected to comprise 1,247,672,923 Ordinary Shares and 257,075,833 B shares. Each Ordinary Share has one voting right and no Ordinary Shares are held in treasury. From Admission, this figure may be used by Shareholders as the denominator for the calculation by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Ends
For further information, please contact:
Enquiries
Tungsten West
Jeff Court, Chief Executive Officer
Phil Povey, Chief Financial Officer
Tel: +44 (0) 1752 278500

(Nominated Adviser and Financial Adviser)

Tel: +44 (0) 207 409 3494

(Financial PR)

Tel: +44(0) 20 7138 3204
Email: tungstenwest@blytheray.com
Follow us on X
@TungstenWest

(Broker)
Andrew Chubb / Matt Hasson / Jay Ashfield
Tel: +44 (0)20 7907 8500

company_30s.what_they_do: Tungsten West Plc extracts and sells tungsten and related minerals. They operate a mine in the United Kingdom and focus on producing high‑purity tungsten for industrial use.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Tungsten is a critical metal in electronics, aerospace and defense. Demand is driven by the growth of electric vehicles and renewable energy infrastructure. The company holds a unique project that could supply a steady stream of this metal to major global buyers.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has recently completed a fundraising round and is in the process of scaling its mining operations. Cash flow is tight, but the company is actively raising capital to keep the project alive and to fund further development.

rns_narrative: The company is busy raising money: a retail offer, a book‑build, and a subscription have all been announced. They are converting B shares and revealing major holdings, signalling that institutional investors are stepping in.
A project update released in February 2026 outlines progress on the mine, indicating that construction milestones are being met and that production could start sooner than previously expected.
The recent results from the general meeting confirm that the company is keeping shareholders informed and is taking steps to improve its balance sheet.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent optimism – the community is mostly quiet, but the few bullish posts suggest that some traders believe the company’s next move could be a surprise breakout.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices point to the new funding round and the projected net present value of the mine. They argue that once the capital is in place, the company could start generating revenue and lift the share price.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter focuses on the company’s thin cash runway and the fact that the project still faces regulatory and technical hurdles. They warn that a delay could push the stock deeper into distress.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been flat, but the recent funding news is beginning to stir interest. The overall mood is cautiously hopeful, with traders waiting to see if the capital raise translates into operational progress.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – the stock is essentially invisible to the broader market, which is a good sign for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: The completion of the retail offer and the confirmation that the capital raise has closed will be a clear sign that the company can fund its project.
Any announcement that the mine has passed a key technical milestone (e.g., environmental approval, first‑stage production) will likely trigger a volume spike.
Positive updates from the project manager about drilling results or ore grade could serve as a trigger for a bounce.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in trading volume and a break of the current low‑range support. Also monitor for any mention of a new investor or a partnership that could accelerate production.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep‑crash recovery. The APEX score suggests tight compression, so a breakout could be imminent once the funding is confirmed and the project moves forward.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen dramatically, likely more than 70% from its peak, giving a huge upside if it rebounds. If the share price recovers to half of its previous high, that would double the current level – a 100% gain. The recent funding round and project progress reduce the risk of a permanent collapse, while the lack of public sentiment means a surprise bounce is plausible.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the capital raise fails or the mine encounters significant delays, the company could run out of cash and be forced to halt operations. The worst‑case scenario is a further 10–15% drop before the company is delisted or goes bankrupt.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 2x current price (100% gain). Potential downside: 10–15% further drop before a rebound. The upside far outweighs the downside, especially given the asymmetric nature of deep‑crash plays.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after the funding round closes and a volume spike confirms investor confidence. Size the position so that a 10% dip would trigger a stop below the recent support level. Use a trailing stop to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear announcement that the mine has passed a key regulatory milestone or that a new investor has committed. Only then consider a small position to test the waters.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a failure to secure the necessary permits or a bankruptcy filing, exit immediately. No further downside is acceptable.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep‑crash tungsten miner that just raised capital – if the project moves forward, the upside is huge and the risk is limited to a small additional dip.
